hub_subtitle: Medical English to welcome guests and present with confidence

lesson.menu_desc: Ask for and offer help politely in a meeting: Can you...? Could you...? Can I...? Add please. Could is softer than can -- "Could you carry this, please?" -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 13 -- Can, Could and Polite Requests
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Graziele Dias | Aula 13 -- Can, Could and Polite Requests

listenings.text: Hello Graziele. I am in a meeting now, and I need a little help. Can you bring the documents to room two, please? And could you carry my laptop? It is on my desk. Please wait for me at the door at three. One more thing: could you explain the schedule to the new nurse? Thank you so much for your help.

Hi Graziele, this is Ana. A quick tip about polite requests. To ask for help, use Can you or Could you and a verb. Could you is softer and more polite than Can you. Always add please. Say: Could you help me, please? To offer help, say: Can I help you? Short, simple, and kind. You can do it!

extra_audio.text: Dr. Carter asks Graziele for help in the meeting. First, he says can you help me, please. Then he asks could you bring the documents. Next, he asks could you carry the laptop. After that, he says please wait at the door. Finally, he asks could you explain the schedule.
